title: ephemerantha ritaeana
intro: Ephemerantha ritaeana, also known as the "Queen of the Andes" or "Rita's orchid," is a species of flowering plant in the orchid family, specifically within the genus Ephemerantha. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Ephemerantha ritaeana are said to include relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to aid in wound healing and reduce fever. Ephemerantha ritaeana is typically prepared as a tea or tincture for its medicinal uses, and it has also been used in folk remedies for its ability to induce sweating and help clear out congestion.
